title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Accounting and Financial Statements: Accounting
Concept: Conceptual framework- Accounting Principles - Accounting
Concepts - Accounting Conventions- Capital and Revenue Expenditure:
Capital and Revenue Receipts - Capital and Revenue Losses - Deferred
Revenue Expenditure- Financial Statements: Understanding and Interpreting
Financial Statements. - Introduction to Financial Statements: Meaning, Types,
and Techniques (Comparative statement, Common size statement, Trend
analysis)

title: Module 2
content:
Accounting Process and Bookkeeping: Assets and Liabilities -types-
Bookkeeping and Record Maintenance- Journal-Ledger-Trial Balance-
Balance Sheet- Final Accounts (simple problems).

title: Module 3
content:
Financial and Cashflow Analysis: Analyzing and Interpreting Financial
Statements (Ratio Analysis)- Funds Flow Analysis - Concept of Funds and
Flow - Statement of Changes in Working Capital - Funds from Operations -
Funds Flow Statement - Uses and Limitations of Funds Flow Statements. Cash
Flow Analysis - Meaning and Significance of Cash Flow Statements.
Preparation of Cash Flow Statement- Difference between fund flow and cash
flow statement.
Class Discussion: Corporate Accounting Fraud- A Case Study of Satyam

title: Module 4
content:
Costing Systems: Definition and Meaning of Cost Accounting- Elements of
Cost - Cost behaviour- Cost allocation- Over Head allocation Types Unit
Costing- Job Costing- Process Costing-Marginal Costing- absorption Costing-
Preparation of Cost Sheet (Simple Problems)- Budgets, ABC Analysis.
